channel.title: NPR Topics: Technology
channel.description: Latest technology news and breakthroughs in technology, science, and industry. Download the NPR Technology podcast and Technology RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Meta considered buying Kalshi before developing its own prediction market app
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg met with Kalshi's CEO last year about a potential deal, but talks did not move forward. Now Meta is making its own prediction market app.
item.content:encoded:
Mark Zuckerberg met with Kalshi's CEO last year about a potential deal, but talks did not move forward. Now Meta is making its own prediction market app.

(Image credit: Patrick T. Fallon/AFP and Aaron Schwartz/Bloomberg via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Red, white and glowing blue: Trump's push for new reactors reaches the finish line
item.description: A program initiated by the Trump administration has allowed small companies to rush their testing of several new nuclear reactor designs. Some worry that safety is being compromised.
item.content:encoded:
A program initiated by the Trump administration has allowed small companies to rush their testing of several new nuclear reactor designs. Some worry that safety is being compromised.

(Image credit: Valar Atomics)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: Study finds Australia's social media ban for children has barely affected access
item.description: Despite Australia promising tougher penalties for a world-first social media ban for children, a new study indicates that six months in, the policy has barely affected youth access.
item.content:encoded: Despite Australia promising tougher penalties for a world-first social media ban for children, a new study indicates that six months in, the policy has barely affected youth access.
item.dc:creator: Kristina Kukolja

item.title: Australia to double potential fines over child social media accounts
item.description: Australia plans to double potential fines for social media platforms, including Facebook and Instagram, who fail to prevent Australian children from holding accounts.
item.content:encoded:
Australia plans to double potential fines for social media platforms, including Facebook and Instagram, who fail to prevent Australian children from holding accounts.

(Image credit: Rick Rycroft)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump administration partially lifts export ban on Anthropic's most advanced AI model
item.description: The U.S. government is asserting a new level of influence over AI, controlling which companies can access Anthropic's new models. OpenAI agreed to let the administration screen users of its new model.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. government is asserting a new level of influence over AI, controlling which companies can access Anthropic's new models. OpenAI agreed to let the administration screen users of its new model.
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Australia plans to strengthen laws banning children from social media
item.description: Observers said on Friday the government is responding to evidence that the ban on young children holding accounts on platforms including Facebook, Instagram and YouTube had failed since it came into force in December year.
item.content:encoded:
Observers said on Friday the government is responding to evidence that the ban on young children holding accounts on platforms including Facebook, Instagram and YouTube had failed since it came into force in December year.

(Image credit: Rick Rycroft)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Despite AI bubble fears, memory chip makers work to fill insatiable demand
item.description: Markets have been on edge about the AI investment boom, but earnings from the biggest U.S. memory chip maker, Micron, signal no end in sight to demand for the microchips at the heart of it all.
item.content:encoded: Markets have been on edge about the AI investment boom, but earnings from the biggest U.S. memory chip maker, Micron, signal no end in sight to demand for the microchips at the heart of it all.
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: U.S. military works on building a better meal for the troops
item.description: The drone-infested battlefields of modern warfare and the need to make troops' loads lighter has the U.S. military looking for new forms of field rations, like ways to make protein on the front lines.
item.content:encoded: The drone-infested battlefields of modern warfare and the need to make troops' loads lighter has the U.S. military looking for new forms of field rations, like ways to make protein on the front lines.
item.dc:creator: Jay Price

item.title: Meta plans to release AI-powered prediction market app, documents show
item.description: The company is building an app separate from Facebook and Instagram where people can wager on the outcome of real-world events, using "play money."
item.content:encoded:
The company is building an app separate from Facebook and Instagram where people can wager on the outcome of real-world events, using "play money."

(Image credit: Kelly Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Star Fox Review: Can't quite teach an old Fox new tricks
item.description: The Switch 2 Star Fox remake comes with high-effort visuals and a fun battle mode, but its campaign feels stuck in the past.
item.content:encoded:
The Switch 2 Star Fox remake comes with high-effort visuals and a fun battle mode, but its campaign feels stuck in the past.

(Image credit: James Mastromarino/Nintendo)
item.dc:creator: James Perkins Mastromarino
